name: Pan Roasted Quail
recipeIngredient: Polenta or mashed potatoes, for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the quail thoroughly inside and out, then place them in a large colander to drain for at least 20 minutes.
2. Pat the quail dry. Stuff the cavity of each bird with 1 slice of pancetta and 1 sage leaf.
3. Put the butter and oil in a large heavy skillet on high heat. When the fat is hot, add all the quail in a single layer and cook until browned on one side. Gradually turn them and continue cooking until they are evenly browned all over. Sprinkle the quail with salt and pepper, then add the white wine and turn the birds once.
4. Let the wine bubble for about 1 minute, then lower the heat to moderate and partially cover the pan. Cook the quail until the meat feels very tender when poked with a fork and comes away from the bone, 45 minutes to 1 hour. Check periodically that there is sufficient juice in the pan to keep the birds from sticking; if not, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water. When the quail are done, transfer them to a warmed serving platter.
5. Add 1/4 cup of water to the skillet. Boil over high heat, scraping the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon to loosen any cooking residues until the water boils away and only a few drops of dark pan juices remain for each bird. Pour the juices over the quail and serve with polenta or mashed potatoes.
